LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS, AND TO DISMISS WITH PREJUDICE OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION RE: FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH SIX SHALL BE RESOLVED THROUGH INDIVIDUAL ARBITRATION. THE CLASS CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED. LITIGATION OF CAUSE OF ACTION SEVEN, THE PAGA CLAIM, SHALL BE STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFF'S OFFER LETTER CONTAINS A VALID AND ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT THAT MUTUALLY BINDS PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT, AND PLAINTIFF AGREED TO BE BOUND BY HER OFFER LETTER. THE AGREEMENT IS GOVERNED BY THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT BECAUSE THE CONTRACT INVOLVES INTERSTATE COMMERCE AND DOES NOT CONTAIN A CALIFORNIA CHOICE OF LAW PROVISION. THE AGREEMENT IS NOT UNCONSCIONABLE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ON AN IND BASIS AND TO DISMISS WITH PREJUDICE/ALT TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION - THIS MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") WAS FILED ON JULY 1, 2014. "BECAUSE THERE IS BUT ONE COMPLAINT IN A CIVIL ACTION, THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS A MOTION DIRECTED TO A PRIOR COMPLAINT." (STATE COMP. INS. FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1124, 1131.) THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT HAS ALREADY FILED A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION DIRECTED AT THE FAC THAT WILL BE HEARD ON SEPTEMBER 16, 2014. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Submitted By Defendant
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by videoconference via Zoom):
Christopher Taylor, Esq. 714-830-0698, christopher.taylor@morganlewis.com of Morgan Lewis Bockius LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MELISSA WOODS' Motion To Tax Costs Submitted By Defendant.

On March 11, 2020, following a bench trial, the court entered judgment in defendant's favor. Defendant filed a timely memorandum of costs seeking recovery of filing and motion fees, court reporter fees, and fees for electronic filing or service in the total amount of $1,547.80. Plaintiff moves to tax $560 in filing and motion fees related to defendant's motions to compel arbitration, arguing that those costs do not relate to the non-arbitrable claims on which the court granted judgment. Plaintiff's motion to tax is denied. As the prevailing party in this action, defendant is entitled as a matter of right to recover all of its costs in this action, including the filing fees at issue. (Code Civ. Proc. 1032(b), 1033.5(a)(1).) Each was reasonably incurred by defendant in this court, and each was necessarily incurred by defendant in compelling arbitration of plaintiff's individual Labor Code claims, which should not have been filed in court in the first place. Plaintiff's contrary position is unsupported by any authority or logic. An arbitrator lacks the authority to award costs incurred in court before an arbitration has commenced.

Plaintiff's position that the court may not decide the motion because she has filed an appeal is mistaken. "[A]n award of attorney fees as costs is a collateral matter which is embraced in the action but is not affected by the order from which an appeal is taken. [Citations.] Consequently, filing of a notice of appeal does not stay any proceedings to determine the matter of costs and does not prevent the trial court from determining a proper award of attorney fees claimed as costs." (Bankes v. Lucas (1992) 9 Cal.App.4th 365, 369.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Mtn To Compel Arb
Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRIAN F. VAN VLECK, TEL. NO. (323)592-3505 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BARBARA J. MILLER OF THE MORGAN LEWIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (949) 399-7121 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC.'s Amended Motion To Compel Arbitration on an individual basis, and to dismiss with prejudice or, in the alternative, to stay proceedings pending arbitration Re: First Amended Complaint - Motion to compel arbitration is Granted. Causes of action one through six shall be resolved through individual arbitration. The class claims are dismissed. Litigation of cause of action seven, the PAGA claim, shall be stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Plaintiff's offer letter contains a valid and enforceable arbitration agreement that mutually binds Plaintiff and Defendant, and Plaintiff agreed to be bound by her Offer Letter. The agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act because the contract involves interstate commerce and does not contain a California choice of law provision. The agreement is not unconscionable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration On An Ind Basis And To Dismiss With Prejudice/Alt To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC's Motion To Compel Arbitration On An Ind Basis And To Dismiss With Prejudice/Alt To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration - This motion is off calendar as moot. A First Amended Complaint ("FAC") was filed on July 1, 2014. "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action, the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint." (State Comp. Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) The Court notes that Defendant has already filed a motion to compel arbitration directed at the FAC that will be heard on September 16, 2014. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
